oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Calf audits – helping clients raise successful calves in the dairy herd
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Richardson, Jonathan
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: audit
calf health
youngstock
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The future of a dairy herd starts in the calf barn. With calves valued at an all-time high, veterinarians play a crucial role in helping farmers maximize calf health and performance that will later pay off in the herd. Ventilation lectures have become the main focus when performing on-farm calf audits, but they are merely the tip of the iceberg. Management factors such as equipment sanitation, water and feed quality, bedding hygiene, seasonal challenges, and record keeping are heavily underem­phasized. The purpose of this review is to share an approach to performing calf audits that span beyond ventilation that you can incorporate into your practice. We will outline where to start, what to look for, and what to track. At the end of the day, the veterinarian performing the audits must understand the goals of the operation and the farmer’s business model. Avoid checking off the boxes when it comes to inspecting calves on your herd visits. Providing thorough audits can help calf raisers reach their goals and become the next revenue stream in your practice.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 47-53
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
